title: AAEP Event: agent.session.errored
description: Announces that the session ended due to an error. MUST be emitted with urgency 'critical'. Defined normatively in Chapter 4 §4.1.3 of the AAEP specification.

urgency.description: MUST be 'critical' for errored sessions. Subscribers MUST announce errors promptly and MUST NOT suppress them under backpressure.

error_category.description: Normative categorization of the error. Determines how subscribers might surface remediation to the user.

summary_normal.description: Human-readable description of the error suitable for direct announcement to the user. Required.

summary_terse.description: Shorter form for terse verbosity. Example: 'Tool timed out.'

summary_detailed.description: Longer form with technical context, possibly including stack trace summary or operational hints.

error_code.description: Producer-defined short error code. Conventionally uppercase with underscores. Useful for log indexing and automated triage.

error_uri.description: URI to documentation or remediation guidance for this specific error. Subscribers MAY surface a 'Learn more' affordance pointing to this URI.

recoverable.description: Whether the session could plausibly succeed if retried. Distinct from error_category: a transient error is recoverable by retry; a requires_user error is recoverable only after user action.

remediation_hint.description: Plain-language suggestion of how the user might address the issue. Subscribers SHOULD include this in announcements when present.

examples.summary_normal: The financial data service did not respond in time. Please try again.
The financial data service did not respond in time. Please try again.
examples.summary_detailed: The fetch_balance tool timed out after 10 seconds connecting to the financial data service. This is usually a temporary issue.
examples.remediation_hint: Try the request again in a few moments.

examples.summary_normal: Your authentication has expired. Please sign in again to continue.
examples.remediation_hint: Sign in to your account, then re-issue your request.
